decision: Target AI-native B2B SaaS vendors at Series A (~20-100 headcount, ~$1-5M ARR) with a live enterprise deal stalled in security/vendor review. Lead vertical financial services, healthcare second. Artifact buyer is founder/CTO below ~50 headcount, GRC/compliance lead from ~50-100.
rationale: l4 upgraded prior inference (b1 conf ~35) to primary firmographics (~65): first enterprise questionnaire lands at late-seed/Series A; $100k+ deals start at Series A; dedicated/fractional GRC lead appears ~50-100 headcount. b2 names financial services as the clearest sector demanding AI-agent-specific evidence beyond SOC 2.

decision: Validate the financial-services-first hypothesis via founder-led discovery (50-100 targets) before settling it as fact; do not invent customer logos.
rationale: b1 vertical rank-order is inference only; l9 found the named-company-plus-dollar deal-loss proof remains thin (usable dated quotes exist but no single post pairs company + deal size + timeline + unblocking artifact).

decision: Keep the EU AI Act as the primary urgency anchor with the live site's two-pronged framing: GPAI obligations + penalties LIVE since 2 Aug 2025; general high-risk + Article 12 logging 2 Aug 2026; Annex III provisionally deferred to 2 Dec 2027 under the Digital Omnibus agreement of 7 May 2026. Do NOT regress to a flat 'August 2026 is settled' claim.
rationale: l8 (primary, Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) confirms the statutory split; regulatory-clock.html already carries the 7 May 2026 Omnibus deferral that the research corpus (e1/e2/l8) lags. Quoting the timeline precisely is itself a reviewer-facing credibility signal.

decision: Reframe urgency from 'your obligation' to 'your buyer's 2026 questionnaire already asks the Article 12 logging question', and address copy to the GRC/compliance owner (Chief Compliance Officer / Head of AI Governance), not a generic security team. Drop any Colorado AI Act urgency.
rationale: e1 cites 2026 agentic RFP templates already asking the logging question; l8 places budget ownership most consistently with the GRC/compliance lead (72% of orgs expect GRC budget growth); e2 confirms Colorado AI Act was repealed and replaced by a narrower ADMT regime effective 1 Jan 2027.

decision: FLAG F2: $750 Signed Readiness Report may read 'too cheap to trust' for a signed deliverable (observed signed/readiness floor ~$1,500-2,500). Price stays LOCKED at $750; mitigate the perception in copy (live methodology, transparency log, offline verify, 'evidence a reviewer can check, not a templated PDF').
rationale: w1 clusters 2026 readiness/gap work at $3-15k; o1 corroborates sub-$1k skepticism; l6 found NO controlled A/B or Van-Westendorp data in the band (anecdote only). Consistent direction, zero controlled data. Authority: pricing is locked and flag-only.

decision: FLAG F1: reconcile the ladder definition. The live audit-pricing.html already ships a Full Readiness $15,000 one-time tier and a Continuous-Plus $3,500/mo tier between $999/mo and $25k that are NOT in the ladder this brief calls 'locked' ($750 / $299 / $999 / $25k / +$10k). Confirm whether $15,000 and $3,500/mo are official.
rationale: Read directly from audit-pricing.html (JSON-LD offers lines 89-95; enterprise tiers lines 282-393). w1 flagged the $999/mo-to-$25k jump as a 'dangerous gap'; l15 confirmed no verbatim buyer demand for a lighter human-reviewed intermediate. The site structurally resolves the gap but disagrees with the brief's locked ladder.

decision: HOLD $25,000 Reviewed Attestation and +$10,000 Deep Red-Team. No change.
rationale: w2: $25k flat sits credibly between a pen test ($10-30k) and SOC 2 Type II / ISO 42001 first-year. l11: named AI red-team vendors are mostly 'contact sales'; $10k add-on sits at the low end (Bishop Fox from ~$10.8k, NCC $60-90k+), possible headroom but no contradiction and no required change.

decision: Prioritize the first-100 motion in CAC-efficiency order: 1 YC/accelerator + warm intro, 2 community (CISO Slacks, AI-founder X), 3 self-serve/PLG, 4 partner/marketplace, 5 content/SEO, 6 cold outbound. Founder-led discovery and a referral flywheel before any sales hire.
rationale: g2 ranks YC batches + founder Slacks cheapest/fastest; l5 attaches benchmark CAC/cycle by channel (YC/warm $50-300, 7-30d; cold outbound $400-2,000, 30-90d); l7 documents the Vanta playbook (founder sold first ~$500k ARR via YC network, first ~600 via word-of-mouth).

decision: Gate the signed report behind the real conversion trigger: an enterprise reviewer/procurement demanding proof in a live deal. Keep the free Scan showing findings + score + a failing line-item, then convert on the trigger. Add a 'Reviewer asking for proof?' CTA path.
rationale: f2 and l13 rank the dominant trigger as external reviewer/procurement demand (then named deal close-date, then EU AI Act deadline). Verbatim: 'Nobody bought SOC 2 audits until enterprise procurement required it.' Site already gates correctly; copy can name the trigger.

decision: BUILD distribution-by-attachment: make kolm reports attachable inside Whistic and Vanta Trust Centers so reviewers see the signed artifact plus auto-mapped SIG/CAIQ answers in one view. Pre-map to the public CSA AI-CAIQ (243 controls) since verbatim SIG AI line-items are non-public.
rationale: l14 specifies the reuse mechanic (Trust Center upload + AI evidence parsing auto-maps controls to SIG/CAIQ with citations back to the signed file); ranked lowest-friction surfaces are Whistic then Vanta. l2 confirms SIG/CAIQ text is non-public except CSA AI-CAIQ.

decision: Size top-of-funnel to first-100 paid reports off freemium proxies: at ~4% free-to-paid, ~2,500 free scans needed (2% -> 5,000; 8% -> 1,250). No company-specific rate exists; treat as a planning proxy, lag 90-180 days.
rationale: f1/l12: no disclosed company-specific free-to-paid rate for comparable security tools; best proxies cybersecurity freemium ~3.6%, PLG B2B median ~9%. l12 supplies the funnel arithmetic.

decision: Treat Cyphrex as the one live direct threat: it now has a PUBLIC pricing page (cyphrex.io/pricing, 25 May 2026) with a $599/mo Verify+ tier shipping 50 signed framework reports/mo. Differentiate kolm on named human co-signer, single-report multi-standard crosswalk, and offline verify with no server and no blockchain/gas in the trust path. Never claim to 'certify' frameworks; kolm MAPS to.
rationale: k1/l3 read the verbatim public page (Starter free, $49, $199, $599 Verify+, custom Enterprise; Solana anchoring; 'cryptographically signed compliance reports'). Overlaps the signed-report + standards-mapping wedge and pressures differentiation.

decision: Lean on pricing transparency as a wedge: flat one-time/flat fees with explicit limits vs the 'contact sales' opacity of Vanta, Drata, SafeBase, Whistic, Conveyor, Secureframe, Sprinto. No exact clone of kolm's full intersection exists yet, but the gap will erode; move fast.
rationale: l3: nearly every competitor gates pricing behind contact-sales; k2: no 2026 product matches kolm's exact intersection (signed readiness/attestation + offline verifiability + inclusion proofs + multi-standard mapping + fixed fee + optional named co-signer).

decision: BUILD the named co-signer supply for the $25k tier from a vetted roster: vCISO.com (published $2,500 fixed 2-week sprint, best price+speed), SideChannel ($3-12k/mo, named ex-CISO depth), Prescient/Johanson (CPA-style $10-20k letter). Co-sign cost loads ~6-15% on $25k; margin stays healthy.
rationale: c1 confirms abundant credentialed supply at $200-350/hr (6-15hr co-sign ~$1.5-3.75k); l1 names suppliers with quoted/reported numbers and a build-vs-buy roster.

decision: BUILD-BEFORE-SCALE the liability scaffold for any named human sign-off: $1-2M E&O (~$2-5k/yr; Hiscox/Chubb/Coalition/At-Bay/Cowbell), ~$1-3k one-time legal drafting, indemnification capped to insurance limits, and verbatim 'readiness not certification / time-boxed / point-in-time' scope language (Blaze disclaimer pattern). FLAG: do not scale the $25k tier before this is in place.
rationale: c2 establishes real liability precedent (Ultramares; US v Hillmer; auditor malpractice) and signer demands; l10 costs the package (~8-20% COGS load on $25k) and notes litigated AI/security sign-off precedent is thin but risk is real.

decision: FLAG: 'Kolm' is arbitrary/registrable but faces moderate-to-high in-lane confusion (Kolm Solutions Kenya IT/security same-lane; Kolm Therapeutics pharma; expired EUIPO 'THE KOLM' class 42). Run a formal clearance search + attorney opinion + an intent-to-use filing before scaling brand spend. Keep the kolm name + three-bar logo.
rationale: n1 open-web scan only (incomplete): arbitrary mark favors registrability in Nice 9/42 but same-lane overlaps exist. Not a copy change; a pre-scale legal gate.

decision: Carry three rebuttals in copy: (1) too-cheap/lightweight -> evidence a reviewer can independently check, not a templated PDF, eight controls across six frameworks; (2) we already have Vanta/Drata + a trust center -> kolm does NOT replace SOC 2 or your trust center, it is the agent-specific evidence that clears THIS deal and every finding maps INTO your existing framework work; (3) a snapshot goes stale -> that is what Continuous is, re-attested weekly or on every deploy.
rationale: o1 ranks these as the top three objections a Series-A buyer raises against a $750 signed report and supplies the rebuttal substance and verbatim risk lines.
